title: Chewy Clay Uruguayan Beef Tagine
description: A hearty, fermented beef tagine with a sweet dark brown sugar glaze, served from a traditional tajine-clay pot for a cozy Uruguayan twist.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a tajine-clay pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the beef cubes and brown on all sides, then set aside.
In the same pot, sauté onion and garlic until fragrant and translucent.
Stir in cumin, smoked paprika, coriander, cinnamon, turmeric, and a pinch of salt and pepper, cooking for 1 minute.
Return the beef to the pot and pour in beef broth, tomato paste, and fermented pickle juice, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 90 minutes, or until the beef is tender and the sauce thickens.
Add the dark soft brown sugar, stir until dissolved, and let the stew cook for an additional 10 minutes to create a sweet glaze.
Adjust seasoning with salt, pepper, and more pickle juice if desired, then remove the lid.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro over the top and serve the tagine hot, directly from the tajine-clay vessel.
Garnish with a few extra pickle slices if you like a tangy contrast.
